title: pholiurus graecus
intro: Pholiurus graecus, also known as Greek club moss, is a type of lycopsid plant belonging to the Pholiurus genus, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and conical shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pholiurus graecus include relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate general fatigue. Pholiurus graecus is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to release its medicinal properties, although modern preparations may also include the plant in capsule or tincture form.
